critical.mandate: Record every significant decision as an ADR in architect-sidecar
PRD must exist before starting architecture
Every technical decision must connect to business value

step.title: Load Upstream Artifacts
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/prd.md — extract requirements
GATE: verify prd.md contains a "## Review Findings Incorporated" section. If missing, HALT — run /gaia-create-prd first to complete adversarial review and PRD refinement.
Read {planning_artifacts}/ux-design.md if available — extract UI requirements

Check for brownfield artifacts: {planning_artifacts}/brownfield-assessment.md and {planning_artifacts}/project-documentation.md. If either exists, load them — these contain existing codebase analysis that must inform architecture decisions even if the PRD is not in brownfield mode.
Check for {planning_artifacts}/threat-model.md. If it exists, load it — identified threats and mitigations must inform the security architecture in Step 7.

step.title: Detect Mode
step.action: Check {planning_artifacts}/prd.md header for "Mode: Brownfield"
step.check: Set {mode} = brownfield. Read brownfield-architecture-template.md from {installed_path}/../../templates/. Also read for cross-references: {planning_artifacts}/api-documentation.md, {planning_artifacts}/event-catalog.md, {planning_artifacts}/dependency-map.md (skip any that don't exist).

step.title: Technology Selection
step.action: Greenfield: select tech stack with rationale for each choice. Consider: team expertise, scalability needs, ecosystem maturity.
Brownfield: discover existing tech stack from project-documentation.md and code scan. Mark ADR status as "Existing" for discovered decisions.

If brownfield artifacts were loaded in Step 1: reference the existing tech stack, constraints, and integration points from project-documentation.md and brownfield-assessment.md when evaluating technology choices. Prefer compatibility with the existing stack unless a change is justified by PRD requirements.
Record decision as ADR in _memory/architect-sidecar/architecture-decisions.md
step.ask: Here is the recommended technology stack with rationale for each choice. Please review — confirm to proceed or request changes before I design the full architecture. [confirm / revise]

step.title: System Architecture
step.action: Greenfield: define component diagram and service boundaries. Describe communication patterns. Record architectural style as ADR.
Brownfield: document as-is architecture with Mermaid diagrams:
— System context diagram (C4 Level 1) using Mermaid C4 syntax
— Container diagram (C4 Level 2) using Mermaid C4 syntax
— 3-5 sequence diagrams for key system flows using Mermaid sequence diagram syntax
— Data flow diagram using Mermaid flowchart syntax
Brownfield: document target architecture for gaps identified in the PRD
Brownfield: create as-is vs target delta table

step.title: Data Architecture
step.action: Design database schema and data model
Define data flow between components
Specify data storage, caching, and replication strategies

step.title: API Design
step.action: Define API endpoint overview
Specify authentication and authorization strategy
Document API versioning approach

step.title: Infrastructure & Cross-Cutting Concerns
step.action: Define deployment topology and environments (dev, staging, prod)
Specify hosting, containerization, and orchestration choices
Define monitoring and logging strategy

Define security architecture: if threat-model.md was loaded, cross-reference identified threats and map each critical/high threat to an architectural mitigation (authentication strategy, authorization model, data encryption at rest and in transit, audit logging, input validation). If no threat model exists, prompt user for key security requirements.
Brownfield: document security architecture, cross-cutting concerns with current state and gaps
Brownfield: define migration strategy from as-is to target architecture
Brownfield: cross-reference api-documentation.md, event-catalog.md, dependency-map.md in the Integration Architecture section

step.title: Architecture Decision Records
step.action: Review all decisions made in steps 3-7
Ensure each significant decision is recorded as ADR
ADR format: title, date, status, context, decision, consequences
Brownfield: mark existing decisions as status "Existing", new gap-related decisions as "Proposed"
For each ADR: add an "Addresses" field listing the FR/NFR IDs it satisfies (e.g., "Addresses: FR-003, NFR-002")
Append all ADRs to _memory/architect-sidecar/architecture-decisions.md
Generate a "Decision → Requirement Mapping" table mapping each ADR to the FR/NFR IDs it addresses. Flag any FR/NFR from the PRD with no corresponding ADR as a coverage gap.

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Greenfield: generate architecture document with: technology stack, system architecture, data architecture, API design, infrastructure plan, ADR references, and Decision → Requirement Mapping table. Brownfield: generate architecture document following brownfield-architecture-template. Must include: C4 Level 1 and Level 2 Mermaid diagrams, 3-5 Mermaid sequence diagrams for key flows, Mermaid data flow diagram, as-is/target delta table, migration strategy, and cross-references to api-documentation.md, event-catalog.md, and dependency-map.md.

step.title: Optional: API Design Review
step.ask: Would you like to review the API design against REST standards? This spawns a subagent in a separate context. Recommended if your architecture includes APIs. (yes / skip)
step.action: If yes: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/tasks/review-api-design.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md. Follow the flow steps EXACTLY. Generate API design findings report."
If skip: API review can be run anytime later with /gaia-review-api

step.title: Adversarial Review
step.action: Spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/tasks/review-adversarial.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md. Follow the task instructions EXACTLY." This is required — findings will be incorporated back into the architecture document.
When subagent returns: verify adversarial-review-{date}.md exists in {planning_artifacts}/

step.title: Incorporate Adversarial Findings
step.action: Read the most recent {planning_artifacts}/adversarial-review-{date}.md — extract critical and high severity findings relevant to architecture
For each critical/high finding: update the architecture — add missing components, revise decisions, strengthen security/scalability, update ADRs
Add a "## Review Findings Incorporated" section to the architecture document listing each finding, its severity, and how it was addressed
step.template-output: Update the architecture document with adversarial review findings incorporated. Critical/high findings lead to architecture revisions. Add "Review Findings Incorporated" section.

next-step.primary: Create risk-based test plan (required before creating epics)
next-step.parallel: Create security threat model (can run in parallel)
